approaches.rationale: Testify is a pure-Go library with a standard go.mod at the root; Gazelle with rules_go can generate go_library/go_test targets across all packages and resolve external deps from go.sum.
approaches.module_bazel:
module(
    name = "testify",
    version = "1.0.0",
)

bazel_dep(name = "rules_go", version = "0.61.1")
bazel_dep(name = "gazelle", version = "0.51.3")

go_sdk = use_extension("@rules_go//go:extensions.bzl", "go_sdk")
go_sdk.download(version = "1.17")

go_deps = use_extension("@gazelle//:extensions.bzl", "go_deps")
go_deps.from_file(go_mod = "//:go.mod")
use_repo(
    go_deps,
    "com_github_davecgh_go_spew",
    "com_github_pmezard_go_difflib",
    "com_github_stretchr_objx",
    "in_gopkg_yaml_v3",
)
approaches.root_build:
load("@gazelle//:def.bzl", "gazelle")

# gazelle:prefix github.com/stretchr/testify
# gazelle:exclude _codegen
# gazelle:exclude _readme-gofmt
gazelle(name = "gazelle")
anticipated_risks: go.mod declares an older go directive (go 1.17) which may not match the SDK default
_codegen has its own go.mod and must be excluded
template files (.go.tmpl) are non-Go and should be ignored by Gazelle
spew internal package has cgo-conditional test files (dumpcgo_test.go / dumpnocgo_test.go) with build tags
use_repo deps list may need adjustment after bazel mod tidy
